widgetData.source_sentence: Conservation of angular momentum in rotating systems
sentences: When no external torque acts on a system, the product of moment of inertia and angular velocity remains constant, leading to effects such as the spin-up of a collapsing neutron star.
Charges in a conductor experience a force proportional to the electric field, causing them to accelerate and establish an electric current through the material.
The uncertainty principle states that it is impossible to simultaneously know the exact position and exact momentum of a particle.

widgetData.source_sentence: Centripetal force in uniform circular motion
sentences: The inward force acting on an object moving in a circular path, directed towards the centre of the circle and given by mv²/r.
The process in which a solid directly changes into a gas without passing through the liquid state.
A thermodynamic quantity representing the total energy of a system, including internal energy and the product of pressure and volume.

widgetData.source_sentence: Interference of light in Young’s double-slit experiment
sentences: The superposition of coherent light waves from two narrow slits produces an alternating pattern of bright and dark fringes on a screen due to constructive and destructive interference.
In a semiconductor, electrons can be thermally excited from the valence band to the conduction band, allowing current to flow.
An ideal gas follows the relation PV = nRT, where P is pressure, V is volume, and T is absolute temperature.

widgetData.source_sentence: The rate of change of magnetic flux in a coil
sentences: According to Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction, a changing magnetic flux through a circuit induces an electromotive force proportional to the rate of change of the flux.
The focal length of a convex lens determines the position and size of the image formed for a given object distance.
A body continues in its state of rest or uniform motion in a straight line unless acted upon by an external unbalanced force.

widgetData.source_sentence: Damped oscillations in a mass-spring system
sentences: The amplitude of oscillation gradually decreases over time due to resistive forces such as friction or air resistance, converting mechanical energy into heat.
Standing waves are formed when two waves of the same frequency and amplitude travel in opposite directions and interfere, producing nodes and antinodes.
The refractive index of a medium is defined as the ratio of the speed of light in vacuum to the speed of light in that medium.
